children.text: prescribing dismissal, suspension, or other adequate punishment for violations.

children.heading: .—Effect of Request for Court-martial
children.label: b .—Effect of Request for Court-martial
children.text: If a cadet who is charged with violating a policy issued under subsection (a), the penalty for which is or may be dismissal from the Academy, requests in writing a trial by a general court-martial, the cadet may not be dismissed for that offense except under sentence of such a court.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: c .—Limitation
children.text: A cadet dismissed from the Academy for hazing or bullying may not be reappointed to the Corps of Cadets, and is ineligible for appointment as commissioned officer in a regular component of the Coast Guard, until the date that is 2 years after the date of the graduation of the class of the cadet.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “a”.1

children.heading: .—Definition of Hazing
children.label: d .—Definition of Hazing
children.text: In developing the policies under subsection (a)(1), the Superintendent shall, to the maximum extent practicable, define the term “hazing” as the unauthorized assumption of authority by a cadet whereby another cadet suffers or is exposed to any cruelty, indignity, humiliation, hardship, or oppression, or the deprivation or abridgement of any right.
